title: If itd be possible and what happened in EVA happen to us
display_title: If itd be possible and what happened in EVA happen to us
wikitext:
[]: "Do you choose to take part in the HCP?" "Yes." ( Reality S****!! Long live the Third Impact!!!! ) 5

[]: If it'd be possible and what happened in EVA happen to us..             Quote Post by JinKazama » Tue May 24, 2005 4:13 am Would you accept the Human Complementation Project or anything alike which will evolve human by artificial means? And if you have the choice will you choose to refuse it? Welcome all your thoughts on this one as well. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Tue May 24, 2005 4:57 am I'd prefer to stay in reality,it's not that it sucks that much! p.s EM has disabled the polls right?

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Tue May 24, 2005 7:51 am I don't like reality that much but I wouldnt want to merge with everyone else either. So I think I'll skip third impact.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue May 24, 2005 7:55 am One size fits all solutions to lifes problems never work. As Shinji discovered, complementation wasn't all it was cracked up to be. An enternity of listening to the self centered, overblown, angst ridden woes of my fellow beings as I tried desperately to retain what little privacy I had remaining is not my idea of a good afterlife.

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Tue May 24, 2005 10:52 am OMF summed it up well. It seems that HCP only shows the dark side. I prefer reality. Besides, I wouldn't want my ex-GFs to know my thoughts.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue May 24, 2005 11:58 am Actually, if HIP went all the way, individual thoughts would no longer exist.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed May 25, 2005 6:29 am The original question JinKazama wrote: Would you accept the Human Complementation Project or anything alike which will evolve human by artificial means?

[]:             Quote Post by JinKazama » Thu May 26, 2005 12:37 am Thanks all of you who posted. Yeah Satre's hell....We are all living in it, so are the characters from Evangelion. I guess I would like to get out of it if given the oppotunity(like what was happening in EVA 8) ) but its just my personal opinion, maybe you'd think differently, but I guess it's just because we live in this world so we'd like to call it reality, we don't have the courage to deny our senses or say we even fear to lose them as if without them we become nothing.

[]:             Quote Post by JinKazama » Thu May 26, 2005 12:46 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: One size fits all solutions to lifes problems never work. As Shinji discovered, complementation wasn't all it was cracked up to be. An enternity of listening to the self centered, overblown, angst ridden woes of my fellow beings as I tried desperately to retain what little privacy I had remaining is not my idea of a good afterlife.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu May 26, 2005 1:24 am Artificially evolve humans, huh? I'd like some things in the genome tweaked. First, give females more default muscle mass. Second, have breasts only swell during lactation. Third, eliminate the nuisance of menstruation -- have the body reabsorb the endometrium instead. Actually, if I had to pick, I'd go with the third. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by DieMuhKuh » Thu May 26, 2005 3:19 am I would definitely stay in reality.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu May 26, 2005 7:47 am @Reichu You mean all the benefits of being a well-ripped body builder without the work (actually in that case point#3 is cessation of cycles because the body fat %age is too low).

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Thu May 26, 2005 1:06 pm Artificially evolve humans, huh? I'd like some things in the genome tweaked. First, give females more default muscle mass. Second, have breasts only swell during lactation. Third, eliminate the nuisance of menstruation -- have the body reabsorb the endometrium instead. Actually, if I had to pick, I'd go with the third. Wink

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu May 26, 2005 1:50 pm Mr. Tines wrote: You mean all the benefits of being a well-ripped body builder without the work (actually in that case point#3 is cessation of cycles because the body fat %age is too low).

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu May 26, 2005 3:18 pm Reichu wrote: Also, I was thinking not so much the cessation of menstruation, but the body dealing with it in a less obnoxious manner. One wonders how on Earth hominids got as far as they did with females dribbling a blood-rich substance on a periodic -- no pun intended -- basis. That's just BEGGING for the predators to come after you.

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Thu May 26, 2005 6:41 pm Mr. Tines wrote: My own favoured minor upgrade would be new teeth every 10-15 years - but that's small potatoes compared with HIP.

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Thu May 26, 2005 6:52 pm Digitalex wrote: Mr. Tines wrote: My own favoured minor upgrade would be new teeth every 10-15 years - but that's small potatoes compared with HIP.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu May 26, 2005 9:12 pm New teeth would be cool. Replacements are not an option. Even though they can survive the atomic explotion, the process of putting them is very expensive, time and money demanding... I'd also like nervous system complete regeneration, and fast recovery even in the case of myelin inflamation. Recombinants take a long, very bothering and sometimes hazardous treatment.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu May 26, 2005 9:51 pm Soluzar wrote: All grown partially from my own living tissue, so that I become THE NEW FLESH!!

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Thu May 26, 2005 10:27 pm Screw bodies, no matter how well built they are; their stability over long periods of time is pathetic. Non-corporeal existence is where it's at.
